name: Lake Granby
description:
Lake Granby is the third-largest body of water in Colorado, located on the western slope of the Continental Divide near Rocky Mountain National Park. The lake sits at 8,280 feet elevation and offers outstanding cold-water fishing in a stunning mountain setting.

Lake trout (mackinaw) exceeding 30 pounds are the premier target, pursued by trolling and jigging in the deep water. Kokanee salmon runs in the fall create exciting fishing along tributary inlets. Rainbow and brown trout round out the fishery. The Sunset Point and Arapaho Bay ramps provide the primary boat access.

name: Dillon Reservoir
description:
Dillon Reservoir is a high-altitude reservoir at 9,017 feet in Summit County, surrounded by the ski towns of Dillon, Silverthorne, and Frisco. The lake serves as Denver's primary water supply while providing excellent fishing.

Arctic char are a unique draw — Dillon is one of the few waters in Colorado where they can be caught. Brown trout exceeding 10 pounds lurk in the deeper water, and the fall kokanee run draws crowds to the Blue River inlet. The Dillon Marina provides the primary launch access with a sailing-only restriction on motors above 20 horsepower.

name: Blue Mesa Reservoir
description:
Blue Mesa Reservoir is Colorado's largest body of water, stretching 20 miles through the upper Gunnison River valley in the Curecanti National Recreation Area. The lake sits at 7,519 feet and holds Colorado's largest kokanee salmon population.

The fall kokanee run is legendary, with millions of fish migrating up the Gunnison River to spawn. Lake trout exceeding 40 pounds have been caught here, and rainbow trout grow to impressive sizes in the nutrient-rich water. Three developed ramps at Elk Creek, Lake Fork, and Stevens Creek provide access to different sections.

name: Eleven Mile Reservoir
description:
Eleven Mile Reservoir is a Gold Medal water in the South Platte drainage, located in the high-altitude grasslands of South Park at 8,600 feet. The lake and its tailwater below the dam offer some of Colorado's finest trout fishing.

Trophy northern pike exceeding 20 pounds add a predator element to the fishery. Rainbow and brown trout are the primary targets, with Eleven Mile producing consistently large fish. The Eleven Mile State Park provides two boat ramps and extensive shore fishing access. Ice fishing is extremely popular during the long winter season.

name: Pueblo Reservoir
description:
Pueblo Reservoir is one of Colorado's most popular warm-water fisheries, located just west of Pueblo in the Arkansas River valley. The lake's lower elevation and warmer temperatures support species not found in the state's mountain reservoirs.

Walleye fishing is the premier draw, with fish commonly exceeding 5 pounds. Smallmouth bass thrive along the rocky north shore, while largemouth bass inhabit the south shore coves. Wipers (white bass/striped bass hybrids) provide explosive topwater action. Two boat ramps at the north and south marinas provide convenient access.

name: Horsetooth Reservoir
description:
Horsetooth Reservoir is a scenic long, narrow reservoir nestled against the foothills west of Fort Collins. The lake stretches nearly 7 miles and provides the most accessible quality fishing in northern Colorado's Front Range.

Smallmouth bass fishing is exceptional along the rocky shoreline and submerged boulders. Walleye are targeted by trollers working the deeper flats, while lake trout inhabit the deepest basins. Three developed ramp areas — South Bay, Inlet Bay, and Satanka Bay — spread fishing pressure across the lake.

name: Chatfield Reservoir
description:
Chatfield Reservoir is one of the most convenient fishing destinations for Denver metro anglers, located at the south edge of the metro area in Chatfield State Park. The reservoir sits at the confluence of the South Platte River and Plum Creek.

Walleye are the premier species, with consistent catches throughout the year. The lake also produces quality largemouth bass, especially in the shallow arms near the river inlets. Annual trout stocking provides excellent spring and fall trout fishing. The well-maintained boat ramp and marina make for easy lake access.

name: Cherry Creek Reservoir
description:
Cherry Creek Reservoir is an urban fishery in the heart of the Denver metro area, located within Cherry Creek State Park. Despite its modest size, the lake consistently produces quality warm-water fishing minutes from downtown Denver.

Walleye are the primary draw, with fish commonly caught in the 3-5 pound range. Wiper fishing provides exciting action during summer feeding frenzies. The shallow, warm water supports healthy populations of bass, crappie, and catfish. The single boat ramp provides access, though shore fishing is productive from the dam and along the south shore.
